skill.name: gpt-image-edit
skill.description: Edit images with OpenAI GPT Image 2 (the `/edit` endpoint of ChatGPT Images 2.0) on RunComfy — bundled with the model's documented prompting patterns so the skill gets sharper output than naive prompting against the same model. Documents GPT Image Edit's strengths (preservation language, multilingual in-image text editing, multi-reference up to 10 images, layout / typography precision), the schema, and when to route to Nano Banana Edit / Flux Kontext / GPT Image 2 t2i instead. Calls `runcomfy run openai/gpt-image-2/edit` through the local RunComfy CLI. Triggers on "gpt image edit", "gpt-image-edit", "chatgpt image edit", "edit with gpt image 2", or any explicit ask to edit with this model.
skill.summary: Edit images with OpenAI GPT Image 2 on RunComfy with preservation language and multilingual text support
skill.author: runcomfy-com

security_audit.summary: Security audit evaluated 184 lines across 1 file. Static analyzer flagged 79 potential issues, but all findings are false positives: backtick patterns are markdown code examples, URL references are documentation links, path placeholders are CLI usage examples, and hidden file access is standard credential storage. The skill invokes the RunComfy CLI for image editing via OpenAI API. No malicious intent, credential exfiltration, or injection vulnerabilities detected.

risk_factor_evidence.reasoning: All 59 backtick detections are markdown code block delimiters for CLI examples (runcomfy run command), not actual shell execution calls. The skill is a documentation file, not executable code.
Hardcoded URLs are legitimate RunComfy service endpoints (runcomfy.com, model-api.runcomfy.net) documented for API usage. These are appropriate for an API skill.
~/.config/runcomfy/token.json is standard CLI credential storage. The skill properly documents this with mode 0600 permissions and supports env var bypass (RUNCOMFY_TOKEN) for CI environments.

medium_findings.title: CLI Command Invocation Patterns
medium_findings.description: Static analyzer detected 59 backtick patterns as potential shell execution. These are markdown code examples showing CLI usage syntax, not actual execution calls.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Confirmed false positive: backticks in markdown delimit code samples. The SKILL.md is a documentation file describing how to invoke the RunComfy CLI, not executable code itself.

low_findings.title: External Service Integration
low_findings.description: Skill connects to external RunComfy Model API (model-api.runcomfy.net) for image editing operations. Properly documented with whitelisted endpoints.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Legitimate API integration. Security section documents outbound endpoints and file size limits (2 GiB cap).

low_findings.title: Local Token Storage
low_findings.description: RunComfy CLI stores API tokens in ~/.config/runcomfy/token.json with mode 0600 permissions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Standard credential storage pattern. CI bypass via RUNCOMFY_TOKEN env var is documented.

content.user_title: Edit images with AI precision using GPT Image 2
content.value_statement: Transform images with precise edits while preserving identity, layout, and multilingual text. GPT Image 2 excels at targeted modifications that maintain brand consistency and translate embedded text across scripts.
actual_capabilities: Edit images while preserving faces, poses, brand marks, and layouts
Rewrite multilingual text in any script (Latin, kana, CJK, Cyrillic, Arabic)
Compose subjects from one image into scenes from another using multi-reference
Precise layout edits with directional language (move headline, swap CTA)
Support up to 10 reference images for complex composition tasks
limitations: Size parameter accepts only 3 fixed values plus auto; other values return 422 error
Long compound prompts cause drift; split into multiple passes for complex edits
Batch consistency across many images is better handled by Nano Banana Edit
Photorealism on portraits may be better with Nano Banana Pro

use_cases.title: Multilingual Ad Localization
use_cases.description: Create multiple language variants of a single source asset. Replace the headline with Japanese kana, Arabic RTL text, or Cyrillic while keeping all other visual elements identical.

use_cases.title: Brand-Safe Hero Image Updates
use_cases.description: Swap headlines, CTAs, or promotional text in brand assets without affecting the main subject, photography, or overall composition. Use preservation language to maintain brand consistency.

use_cases.title: Product Photography Composition
use_cases.description: Compose subjects from product photos into lifestyle scenes. Combine the product from one image with the lighting and background from another while preserving subject identity.

prompt_templates.title: Simple Background Swap
prompt_templates.prompt: Keep the [main subject] and [brand elements] exactly as in the input. Replace the background with [description of new background].
prompt_templates.scenario: When user wants to change the backdrop of a photo while keeping the subject intact

prompt_templates.title: Multilingual Text Replacement
prompt_templates.prompt: Keep the photograph, layout, and brand mark exactly as in the input. Replace only the in-image headline. The new headline reads "[TEXT]" in [script name], same position and font weight as before.
prompt_templates.scenario: When user wants to translate embedded text in marketing materials

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Reference Composition
prompt_templates.prompt: Compose [element from image 1] into [scene from image 2]. Match the [lighting/color palette] from image 2. Keep [identity elements] from image 1 unchanged.
prompt_templates.scenario: When combining elements from multiple source images

prompt_templates.title: Layout Repositioning
prompt_templates.prompt: Keep [preserved elements] unchanged. Move the [element] from [original position] to [new position].
prompt_templates.scenario: When user wants to reposition visual elements within the frame

output_examples.input: Edit a product photo to change the background to a clean white studio sweep while keeping the product and shadow identical
output_examples.output: Returns the edited image with the new background, preserving the product appearance, angle, and subtle shadow details

output_examples.input: Replace the English headline in a poster with Japanese text reading '今日のおすすめ' in bold kana
output_examples.output: Returns the poster with Japanese text rendered in the same position and font weight as the original headline

output_examples.input: Compose the model from photo1 into the kitchen scene from photo2, matching the warm window lighting
output_examples.output: Returns a composite image with the person from the first photo positioned naturally in the kitchen scene with matching lighting

best_practices: Lead prompts with preservation goals: explicitly state what to keep unchanged before describing the edit
Quote in-image text exactly when replacing it; paraphrase causes text to differ from intent
Use directional language for spatial edits: "move from top-right to bottom-center" works better than vague instructions
anti_patterns: Avoid long compound edits (A and B and C and D); split into sequential passes to prevent drift
Do not skip preservation language; model may subtly rewrite faces, brands, or framing without explicit guidance
Do not paraphrase multilingual text; quote the exact characters and name the script for accurate rendering

faq.question: How does this skill differ from text-to-image generation?
faq.answer: This skill edits existing images by modifying specific elements while preserving others. Text-to-image generates new images from scratch. Use this for targeted changes to photos, marketing assets, or product images.

faq.question: What is the difference between GPT Image Edit and Nano Banana Edit?
faq.answer: GPT Image Edit excels at identity preservation and multilingual text editing with up to 10 references. Nano Banana Edit is better for batch consistency across many images (up to 20) and photorealism on portraits.

faq.question: Can I edit multiple images at once?
faq.answer: You can pass up to 10 reference images in a single edit request for composition purposes. For batch editing multiple separate images consistently, consider the Nano Banana Edit skill instead.

faq.question: What size formats are supported?
faq.answer: The API supports three fixed sizes (1024x1024, 1024x1536, 1536x1024) plus auto which preserves the input image ratio. Other sizes will return a 422 error.

faq.question: How do I preserve brand elements in an edit?
faq.answer: Include explicit preservation language at the start of your prompt: "Keep the face, pose, and brand mark unchanged." The model prioritizes what's stated up front in the prompt.

faq.question: What are the exit codes and when do they occur?
faq.answer: Exit code 0 means success. Code 64 indicates bad CLI arguments, 65 means invalid JSON or schema mismatch, 69 indicates upstream 5xx errors, 75 means retryable timeout or rate limit, and 77 means authentication failed.
